visualizations.title: Divisibility Rules Summary Table
visualizations.description: Quick reference guidelines for divisibility testing.

spec.main_latex: \begin{array}{|c|l|} \hline \text{Divisor} & \text{Rule} \\ \hline 2 & \text{Ends in 0, 2, 4, 6, 8} \\ 3 & \text{Sum of digits is divisible by 3} \\ 4 & \text{Last 2 digits divisible by 4} \\ 5 & \text{Ends in 0 or 5} \\ 6 & \text{Divisible by both 2 and 3} \\ 9 & \text{Sum of digits is divisible by 9} \\ 10 & \text{Ends in 0} \\ \hline \end{array}

steps.explanation: Rules for 2, 4, and 8 depend entirely on the final digits.
Rules for 3 and 9 require adding the digits together.

visualizations.title: Testing Divisibility of 168
visualizations.description: Watch the digit sum check: 1 + 6 + 8 = 15. Since 15 is divisible by 3 but not 9, 168 follows the same rules.
